mechanism_narrative: A high federal funds rate raises the discount rate on future equity cash flows and tightens financing conditions, so higher FEDFUNDS levels predict lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher FEDFUNDS levels are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations, or if the FEDFUNDS return coefficient is nonnegative over two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5% while CPIAUCSL remains elevated, markets price a tighter-for-longer policy path that compresses equity valuation multiples and produces negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns more often than untriggered observations by at least 5 percentage points, or if the interaction coefficient between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL is positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE accompanied by weak PAYEMS growth signals deteriorating labor income and rising recession risk, causing investors to lower earnings expectations and producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months while PAYEMS 3-month growth slows
failure_condition: Reject if triggered labor-deterioration episodes have median 20-day S&P 500 returns greater than zero, or if the signal hit rate for negative returns is below 50% over two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in RSAFS indicates resilient household demand and supports near-term corporate revenue expectations, so stronger retail sales growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum is followed by nonpositive average 20-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward testing, or if the RSAFS momentum coefficient is negative in more than half of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO signals weakening production volumes and margin pressure for cyclical firms, leading analysts to cut earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its trailing 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside crossings are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional average return.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST below recent norms signals weakening rate-sensitive demand and lower future construction activity, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its trailing 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside crossings do not reduce subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns relative to baseline, or if triggered returns are positive in more than 60% of walk-forward instances.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation momentum decelerates while PAYEMS expands and UNRATE stays low, the economy fits a soft-landing pattern that supports earnings and lowers rate-risk premia, producing positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation momentum declines while PAYEMS rises and UNRATE is at or below 4.3%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered soft-landing observations have average 20-day S&P 500 returns below zero, or if the joint CPIAUCSL-PAYEMS-UNRATE signal fails to outperform single-series signals by at least 1 percentage point annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDPC1 growth combined with a high FEDFUNDS level signals demand resilience under restrictive policy, which supports earnings estimates enough to produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly growth is positive and FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the GDPC1-positive and FEDFUNDS-above-4.5% trigger is followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations, or if the interaction coefficient is negative across two consecutive walk-forward windows.
